title: Is anyone geting bored of this show
display_title: Is anyone geting bored of this show
wikitext:
[]: Is anyone geting bored of this show?             Quote Post by The pain that is life » Mon May 09, 2005 4:42 pm Is anybody bored of evangelion? Honestly im sorry to tell you all this but well.... I sort of am I own the entire series on dvd and I think i have watched it so many times im well starting to hate it. :(

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 09, 2005 4:53 pm Yep, though I do plan to get the whole set on dvd before I jump ship. Try www.gamesekai.com if your looking for a net place to lurk. Eva's a great show but after a wile you need somthing else. Contrary to popular theory this is not the be all and end all of anime. PS you can edit your posts so you don't need to double post its concidered rude.

[]:             Quote Post by The pain that is life » Mon May 09, 2005 4:56 pm man I swear to god every time I watch Eangelion I feel like my head is gonna explode... its just not fair!

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 09, 2005 4:59 pm I tend to be n00b frendly and try to guide your kind into becoming good posters. You will find others here less tollerent of spam and spammers.

[]:             Quote Post by The pain that is life » Mon May 09, 2005 5:01 pm It bring joy to my heart that your here to help, but i would prefer people that people did not call me a noob!

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 09, 2005 5:03 pm I tryed >sigh<

[]:             Quote Post by The pain that is life » Mon May 09, 2005 5:04 pm Well I will be honest i'm a noob and im proud of it!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 09, 2005 5:06 pm The pain that is life wrote: Is anybody bored of evangelion? Honestly im sorry to tell you all this but well.... I sort of am I own the entire series on dvd and I think i have watched it so many times im well starting to hate it. :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by The pain that is life » Mon May 09, 2005 5:10 pm you know what I think you are right, but theres one more thing bothering me and that thing is well, will eva ever go on I was really pissed becuase it ended so fast, for me that is.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon May 09, 2005 5:29 pm The pain that is life wrote: It bring joy to my heart that your here to help, but i would prefer people that people did not call me a noob!

[]:             Quote Post by The pain that is life » Mon May 09, 2005 5:34 pm troll, never heard of that one

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon May 09, 2005 8:57 pm but i would prefer people that people did not call me a noob!

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Mon May 09, 2005 9:10 pm Watching Eva is like having sex, and we all know how boring that gets.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon May 09, 2005 9:37 pm When it comes to discussion of the show, I find that I make more inaccurate points than most. Yet I've come to a point where I feel like I don't ever have to watch it ever again in order to have a handle on the whole of its existence, however much that may vary from reality. I'm not even really attracted to discussion anymore. I may not remember everything perfectly, but like Niles Crane said on Frasier , "I realized there was no word I couldn't spell." Sometimes you just have to decide to use your moments for something else, and stick with the Eva in your head to get you through. That's how you learn to know something in your heart rather than your head. I look forward to returning to it many years from now and seeing how much I screwed it up or improved it.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 09, 2005 9:47 pm Dave wrote: Watching Eva is like having sex, and we all know how boring that gets.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Mon May 09, 2005 10:00 pm Well, I've just gotten into Evangelion over the last two months after seeing the first few episodes 5 years ago. I think I will eventually get bored of it but not to the point where I dismiss it totally. I treat Eva like a good book. It's worth another read every once in a while. The story and discussions that it spawned are unlike any other anime I have seen in the 20 years I have been into this genre. I think that's what makes it an instant classic for me. The characters and story are memorable. I do have a habit of immersing myself in anything that interests me to certain extremes. I've gone out and purchased most of the DVDs (waiting for my recent order of Platinum versions to arrive), started collecting figures, and started hunting down the art books. I'm sure this peak interest will eventually wane but I'm pretty sure I'll still have an interest in it because of the complexity surrounding the characters and story.

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Mon May 09, 2005 10:31 pm PS you can edit your posts so you don't need to double post its concidered rude.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Mon May 09, 2005 10:49 pm @ Hexon The beauty of the world is that you don't have settle for just "the Eva in your head"; the sad truth is that you can never watch it for the first time more than once. We may bore of it, but I for one will never wish I hadn't seen it. Nor will I be so disrespectful as to rely upon my thoughts of it, rather than it itself. I'd take the moment I first saw Rei over the memory any day, and so I shall.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon May 09, 2005 10:58 pm Dave wrote: @ Hexon The beauty of the world is that you don't have settle for just "the Eva in your head"; the sad truth is that you can never watch it for the first time more than once. We may bore of it, but I for one will never wish I hadn't seen it. Nor will I be so disrespectful as to rely upon my thoughts of it, rather than it itself. I'd take the moment I first saw Rei over the memory any day, and so I shall.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Mon May 09, 2005 11:18 pm "Our hearts will be in unrest until they rest in thee" Who said that?
